chapter_title.en: The Call to Prayer and the Establishment of Prayer and the Reward of the Muezzins

text: 879 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ زُرَارَةُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِذَا أُقِيمَتِ اَلصَّلاَةُ حَرُمَ اَلْكَلاَمُ عَلَى اَلْإِمَامِ وَ عَلَى أَهْلِ اَلْمَسْجِدِ إِلاَّ فِي تَقْدِيمِ إِمَامٍ »

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.879 - Zurarah narrated from Imam Abu Ja'far (as) that He said:
"When the iqamah (second call to prayer) is pronounced, speaking becomes prohibited for the Imam and the people of the mosque, except in the case of appointing an Imam."
